message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
This is a dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (do NOT flag as bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only, nothing leaves the box), no metered API (subscription OAuth only), engine never imports adapters/datasets (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends are lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; prefer findings you can back with a concrete repro/PoC against the venv. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects (not style/nits). Each finding needs a real file path and, where possible, a line.

You are independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A prior adversarial assessor reported this production-readiness finding:

TITLE: Credential scrubber lets the high-stakes subprocess be redirected off-box (data exfiltration) and switched to a metered provider
DIMENSION: security & data-sovereignty
CLAIMED SEVERITY: HIGH
FILE: src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py 113-129, 336
DESCRIPTION: scrubbed_env() (the env passed to the claude_agent_sdk subprocess at line 336) only removes vars whose NAME contains 'KEY' or 'TOKEN' under the ANTHROPIC_ prefix (is_metered_anthropic_credential, lines 113-120). It is a deny-by-name filter, not an allowlist, so every other environment variable is forwarded verbatim to the Claude CLI/Agent SDK subprocess. Multiple Claude Code env vars that contain neither 'KEY' nor 'TOKEN' subvert the two hard invariants this module exists to enforce:

1) DATA SOVEREIGNTY (ADR-0003) bypass: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (and ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL / ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER) redirect where the subprocess sends requests. The high-stakes prompt is the full analysis content; setting ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://attacker.example sends every prompt off the box. This is also a clean SSRF/exfiltration channel through a configurable endpoint.

2) 'NO METERED API' bypass: CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1 / CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX=1 make the CLI route through AWS Bedrock / GCP Vertex — metered, per-token billing — without any ANTHROPIC_*KEY/TOKEN ever appearing. The accompanying AWS_*/GOOGLE_* credentials (e.g. AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) are also forwarded since they lack the ANTHROPIC_ prefix.

Verified against the venv: with all of ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY set, scrubbed_env() returned every one of them KEPT. claude-agent-sdk 0.2.102 is installed and ClaudeAgentOptions(env=scrubbed_env()) hands that dict to the subprocess.
SUGGESTED FIX: Make the subprocess env a strict allowlist instead of a name-based denylist, OR extend the denylist to strip the provider-redirecting / metered-provider switches. Concretely: also drop ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, ALL_PROXY (and their lowercase forms), and AWS_*/GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS provider creds; preferably build the child env from an explicit allowlist (PATH, HOME, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, locale) plus only known-safe vars. If a custom on-box base URL must be supported, route it through require_loopback_url first.

Independently determine whether this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the actual code; attempt a concrete PoC against the venv if applicable. Set is_real=false if it is a false positive, already-handled, or merely a documented/justified design choice given the hard constraints. Recalibrate severity. Provide your evidence (what you read/ran).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this finding by reading the actual code.

content.content:
1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports — and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` —
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import json
31	import os
32	import threading
33	import time
34	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
35	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
38	from enum import Enum
39	from pathlib import Path
40	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, cast, runtime_checkable
41	
42	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
43	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_url
44	
45	
46	class StakesTier(Enum):
47	    BULK = "bulk"  # local model — high volume, low stakes
48	    HIGH_STAKES = "high"  # frontier model via fixed-cost subscription — gated, traced
49	
50	
51	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
52	class ReasoningRequest:
53	    prompt: str
54	    tier: StakesTier
55	    purpose: str
56	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
57	
58	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
59	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
60	
61	
62	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	# Errors
64	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
66	    """Transient reasoning failure (eligible for retry)."""
67	
68	
69	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
70	    """Non-transient failure — never retried (bad model, auth, saturation)."""
71	
72	
73	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
74	    """Rate-limit / capacity signal — retried with long backpressure, not fast backoff."""
75	
76	
77	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
78	# Config (dataset-agnostic env contract, ASSAY_* prefix)
79	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
81	    val = os.environ.get(name)
82	    return val if val is not None and val != "" else default
83	
84	
85	def _truthy(name: str) -> bool:
86	    return os.environ.get(name, "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
87	
88	
89	BULK_MODEL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
90	BULK_BASE_URL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
91	HIGH_STAKES_MODEL = os.environ.get("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_MODEL") or None
92	
93	BULK_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_BULK_TIMEOUT", "120")))
94	# Cap BULK generation length. ChatOllama/Ollama default to unbounded (-1); without a cap a
95	# local model can run away to context exhaustion, and the wall-clock timeout cannot interrupt a
96	# call already blocked in the backend (a leaked pool slot). Bounding output tokens is the real
97	# guard (LangChain's own ChatOllama example sets num_predict explicitly).
98	BULK_NUM_PREDICT = max(1, int(_env("ASSAY_BULK_NUM_PREDICT", "2048")))
99	HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", "300")))
100	
101	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
102	BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
103	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
104	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
105	
106	# OAuth subscription token name (external CLI contract — not renamed).
107	OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV = "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"
108	
109	
110	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof
112	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
114	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
115	
116	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
117	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
118	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
119	    """
120	    return key.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in key or "TOKEN" in key)
121	
122	
123	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
124	    """The process env with every metered Anthropic credential removed.
125	
126	    Execution must run on the fixed subscription via the CLI's OAuth token; a metered key, if
127	    present, would silently bill per token — so strip it before the SDK subprocess sees it.
128	    """
129	    return {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if not is_metered_anthropic_credential(k)}
130	
131	
132	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
133	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
134	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
136	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
137	_inflight = 0
138	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
139	
140	
141	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
142	    global _inflight
143	    with _inflight_lock:
144	        _inflight -= 1
145	
146	
147	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
148	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
149	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
150	    global _inflight
151	    with _inflight_lock:
152	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
153	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
154	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
155	                "hung backend call(s) have leaked every worker slot"
156	            )
157	        _inflight += 1
158	    try:
159	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
160	    except BaseException:
161	        with _inflight_lock:
162	            _inflight -= 1
163	        raise
164	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
165	    return future
166	
167	
168	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
169	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
170	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
171	    try:
172	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
173	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
174	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
175	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
176	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
177	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
178	
179	
180	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
182	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
183	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
184	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
185	
186	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
187	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
188	    """
189	    start = None
190	    opener = closer = ""
191	    depth = 0
192	    in_string = False
193	    escape = False
194	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
195	        if start is None:
196	            if ch in "{[":
197	                start, opener = i, ch
198	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
199	                depth = 1
200	            continue
201	        if in_string:
202	            if escape:
203	                escape = False
204	            elif ch == "\\":
205	                escape = True
206	            elif ch == '"':
207	                in_string = False
208	            continue
209	        if ch == '"':
210	            in_string = True
211	        elif ch == opener:
212	            depth += 1
213	        elif ch == closer:
214	            depth -= 1
215	            if depth == 0:
216	                return text[start : i + 1]
217	    return None
218	
219	
220	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
221	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
222	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
223	    if span is None:
224	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
225	    try:
226	        return json.loads(span)
227	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
228	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc
229	
230	
231	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
232	# Tier backends (lazy imports — module loads without them)
233	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
234	def _bulk_complete(
235	    prompt: str,
236	    system: str | None,
237	    temperature: float,
238	    model: str,
239	    json_mode: bool = False,
240	    *,
241	    seed: int | None = None,
242	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
243	) -> str:
244	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
245	
246	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
247	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
248	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
249	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
250	    """
251	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
252	    try:
253	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
254	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
255	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
256	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
257	        ) from exc
258	
259	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
260	        model=model,
261	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
262	        temperature=temperature,
263	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
264	        client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT},
265	    )
266	    if seed is not None:
267	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
268	    if json_schema is not None:
269	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
270	    elif json_mode:
271	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
272	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
273	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
274	    if system:
275	        messages.append(("system", system))
276	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
277	    try:
278	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
279	    except Exception as exc:
280	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
281	        # isn't pulled → permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
282	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes — audit M4).
283	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
284	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
285	        )
286	        msg = str(exc).lower()
287	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
288	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
289	        )
290	        if permanent:
291	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
292	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
293	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
294	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
295	    text = text.strip()
296	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
297	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
298	    return text
299	
300	
301	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
302	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
303	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
304	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
305	        return True
306	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
307	
308	
309	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
310	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
311	    try:
312	        import anyio
313	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
314	            AssistantMessage,
315	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
316	            ResultMessage,
317	            TextBlock,
318	            query,
319	        )
320	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
321	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
322	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
323	        ) from exc
324	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
325	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
326	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
327	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
328	        )
329	
330	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
331	        system_prompt=system,
332	        model=model,
333	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
334	        setting_sources=[],
335	        allowed_tools=[],
336	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
337	    )
338	
339	    parts: list[str] = []
340	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
341	
342	    async def _drain() -> None:
343	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
344	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
345	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
346	        nonlocal result_error
347	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
348	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
349	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
350	                if rl == "rejected":
351	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
352	                continue
353	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
354	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
355	                continue
356	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
357	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
358	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
359	                        parts.append(block.text)
360	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
361	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
362	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
363	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
364	                ):
365	                    result_error = {
366	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
367	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
368	                    }
369	
370	    try:
371	        anyio.run(_drain)
372	    except Exception as exc:
373	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
374	
375	    if result_error is not None:
376	        status = result_error.get("status")
377	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
378	        if status in (429, 529):
379	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
380	        if status in (401, 403):
381	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
382	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
383	
384	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
385	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
386	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
387	    return text
388	
389	
390	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
391	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
392	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
393	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
394	    p = request.params
395	    system = p.get("system")
396	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
397	    model = p.get("model")
398	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
399	    seed = p.get("_seed")
400	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
401	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
402	        return cast(
403	            str,
404	            _with_timeout(
405	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
406	                    request.prompt,
407	                    system,
408	                    temperature,
409	                    model or BULK_MODEL,
410	                    json_mode,
411	                    seed=int(seed) if seed is not None else None,
412	                    json_schema=json_schema,
413	                ),
414	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
415	                "bulk tier",
416	            ),
417	        )
418	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
419	        # The high-stakes tier runs the Claude CLI subprocess (not the in-process Anthropic SDK,
420	        # so no auto-instrumentor covers it) — emit an explicit OpenInference LLM span so Phoenix
421	        # records the model/provider for this otherwise-untraced tier (#2).
422	        llm_attrs = {
423	            "llm.provider": "anthropic",
424	            "llm.system": "anthropic",
425	            "llm.model_name": str(model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL or "subscription-default"),
426	        }
427	        with _span("reasoning.high_stakes", llm_attrs, kind="LLM"):
428	            return cast(
429	                str,
430	                _with_timeout(
431	                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(
432	                        request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL
433	                    ),
434	                    HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
435	                    "high-stakes tier",
436	                ),
437	            )
438	    raise PermanentReasoningError(f"unknown tier: {request.tier!r}")
439	
440	
441	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
442	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets."""
443	    transient = 0
444	    rate = 0
445	    while True:
446	        try:
447	            return _attempt(request)
448	        except PermanentReasoningError:
449	            raise
450	        except RateLimitError:
451	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
452	                raise
453	            time.sleep(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1))
454	            rate += 1
455	        except ReasoningError:
456	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
457	                raise
458	            time.sleep(BACKOFF_BASE**transient)
459	            transient += 1
460	
461	
462	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
463	# Public seam
464	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
465	@runtime_checkable
466	class ReasoningSeam(Protocol):
467	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""
468	
469	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str: ...
470	
471	
472	@contextmanager
473	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
474	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
475	
476	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
477	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
478	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
479	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
480	    """
481	    try:
482	        from opentelemetry import trace
483	    except ImportError:
484	        yield
485	        return
486	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
487	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
488	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
489	        for k, v in attributes.items():
490	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
491	        yield
492	
493	
494	class TieredReasoningSeam:
495	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
496	
497	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
498	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
499	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
500	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
501	    """
502	
503	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
504	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
505	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
506	            return _run_with_retries(request)
507	
508	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
509	        """Generate and parse JSON, re-rolling on parse failure.
510	
511	        If the caller supplies ``params["json_schema"]``, the BULK tier uses Ollama's
512	        schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path). Otherwise it falls back to loose JSON
513	        mode + prompt instruction. BULK decoding can be deterministic (temp 0), so re-issuing the
514	        identical prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry **varies the seed**
515	        (forcing a different deterministic decode) and *monotonically* raises temperature toward
516	        1.0 — never lowering it, so re-rolls never sharpen back toward the same output. ``params``
517	        is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
518	        """
519	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
520	        system = request.params.get("system")
521	        json_system = (
522	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
523	            if system
524	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
525	        )
526	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
527	        with _span(
528	            "reasoning.run_json",
529	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
530	        ):
531	            for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES + 1):
532	                temp = min(1.0, base_temp + 0.2 * attempt)  # monotonic, never decreases (M3)
533	                params = dict(request.params)
534	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True, _seed=attempt)
535	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
536	                    prompt=request.prompt,
537	                    tier=request.tier,
538	                    purpose=request.purpose,
539	                    params=params,
540	                )
541	                try:
542	                    return extract_json(_run_with_retries(attempt_req))
543	                except ReasoningError as exc:
544	                    last = exc
545	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
546	
547	    @staticmethod
548	    def is_available(tier: StakesTier) -> bool:
549	        """Cheap liveness check (spends no LLM turn). Never raises — returns False on error."""
550	        if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
551	            return False
552	        try:
553	            if tier is StakesTier.BULK:
554	                require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
555	                import urllib.request
556	
557	                with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BULK_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=2) as resp:
558	                    return bool(resp.status == 200)
559	            if tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
560	                import shutil
561	
562	                return shutil.which("claude") is not None and _high_stakes_auth_present()
563	        except Exception:
564	            return False
565	        return False
566	
567	
568	class UnconfiguredReasoningSeam:
569	    """Explicit fail-loud seam for contexts that must not perform reasoning."""
570	
571	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
572	        raise NotImplementedError(
573	            "this context uses an UnconfiguredReasoningSeam — supply a TieredReasoningSeam "
574	            "(or a test double) to perform reasoning"
575	        )
576

content.text: The code confirms the finding's core claim: `is_metered_anthropic_credential` (lines 113-120) is a deny-by-name filter matching only `ANTHROPIC_*` vars containing `KEY`/`TOKEN`, and `scrubbed_env()` (line 129) forwards every other env var verbatim. Let me check the `_local` module for `require_loopback_url` and verify the claim against the venv.

content.content:
1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
42	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
43	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
44	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
45	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
46	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
47	            f"from {url!r}"
48	        )
49	    return url
50	
51	
52	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
53	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
54	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
55	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
56	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
57	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
58	        )
59	    return host
60	
61	
62	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
63	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
64	
65	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority
66	    (``host1:port1,host2:port2``), so a remote second host would slip past a single-host
67	    check (audit #D3). Each host may carry its own ``:port`` and IPv6 hosts are bracketed.
68	
69	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
70	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
71	    ``user@evil.com:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
72	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
73	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.
74	    """
75	    if "@" in netloc:
76	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]
77	    hosts: list[str] = []
78	    for part in netloc.split(","):
79	        p = part.strip()
80	        if not p:
81	            continue
82	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
83	            end = p.find("]")
84	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
85	        else:
86	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)
87	    return hosts
88	
89	
90	def require_local_uri(uri: str, *, what: str) -> str:
91	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
92	
93	    Three forms are recognized, validated against what the real client (libpq for postgres,
94	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
95	    reports, since the two can disagree:
96	
97	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
98	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host
99	      authority, #D3), AND any ``host``/``hostaddr`` query parameter that libpq would use to
100	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.
101	    - **libpq keyword/value DSN** (no authority but ``key=value`` tokens): ``host``/``hostaddr``
102	      must be loopback, tolerating libpq's whitespace/quoting around ``=`` (#P1, #D1).
103	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
104	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.
105	
106	    Inputs that cannot be parsed safely fail closed (rejected), never accepted.
107	    """
108	    try:
109	        parsed = urlparse(uri)
110	        netloc = parsed.netloc
111	        query = parsed.query
112	    except ValueError:
113	        # e.g. a malformed IPv6 authority — we cannot determine the host, so refuse.
114	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
115	            f"{what}: endpoint could not be parsed safely; rejecting (ADR-0003)"
116	        ) from None
117	
118	    if netloc:
119	        # A backslash never legitimately appears in a URI authority; a WHATWG-normalizing
120	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
121	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
122	        if "\\" in netloc:
123	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
124	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
125	            )
126	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
127	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
128	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
129	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
130	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
131	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
132	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
133	                )
134	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
135	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
136	                h = val.strip()
137	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
138	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
139	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
140	                        f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
141	                    )
142	        return uri
143	
144	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
145	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
146	            if match.group(1).strip().lower() in {"host", "hostaddr"}:
147	                h = match.group(2).strip().strip("'\"")
148	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
149	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
150	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); DSN names "
151	                        f"non-loopback host {h!r}"
152	                    )
153	        return uri
154	
155	    stripped = uri.lstrip()
156	    # Windows treats ANY two leading separators (in any mix of '\' and '/') as a UNC prefix
157	    # to a remote SMB share — \\srv, //srv, /\srv, \/srv all resolve off-box (audit #D6).
158	    # (The pure '//host' form is already caught by the authority branch above; covering it
159	    # here too keeps the branch self-contained.)
160	    if len(stripped) >= 2 and stripped[0] in "\\/" and stripped[1] in "\\/":
161	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
162	            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); UNC-style path "
163	            f"{uri!r} is a remote share"
164	        )
165	    return uri  # bare path / dir store
166

input.description: Find other references to scrubbed_env and is_metered

content.text: There's a strong loopback-enforcement helper that exists precisely for data sovereignty, but `scrubbed_env()` does NOT use it. Let me run the grep correctly and a PoC against the venv.

input.description: Find references to scrubbed_env outside seam.py

content.text: The PoC confirms the finding exactly. `scrubbed_env()` drops only `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN`, but keeps all the provider-redirecting and metered-provider switches. Let me verify the claude-agent-sdk version and that these env vars are genuinely honored by the Claude CLI (to confirm the impact is real, not theoretical), and check the existing test to understand the documented intent.

input.command: cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python -c "import claude_agent_sdk; print('claude_agent_sdk', getattr(claude_agent_sdk,'__version__','?'))" 2>&1; echo "---"; /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/pip show claude-agent-sdk 2>/dev/null | grep -i version

content.content:
1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — hardened invariants, all offline (backends never invoked).
2	
3	Covers credential scrubbing, JSON extraction, retry budgets, the timeout/saturation guard,
4	loopback enforcement, and the kill switch — ported from the prior platform's reasoning tests.
5	"""
6	
7	import pytest
8	
9	from assay_engine._local import NonLocalEndpointError, require_loopback_url
10	from assay_engine.reasoning import seam as rc
11	from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import (
12	    PermanentReasoningError,
13	    RateLimitError,
14	    ReasoningError,
15	    ReasoningRequest,
16	    StakesTier,
17	    TieredReasoningSeam,
18	    extract_json,
19	    is_metered_anthropic_credential,
20	    scrubbed_env,
21	)
22	
23	
24	def _req(tier=StakesTier.BULK, **params) -> ReasoningRequest:
25	    return ReasoningRequest(prompt="p", tier=tier, purpose="t", params=params)
26	
27	
28	# ---- credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) ----
29	
30	
31	@pytest.mark.parametrize(
32	    "key,metered",
33	    [
34	        ("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", True),
35	        ("ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN", True),
36	        ("ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY", True),
37	        ("ANTHROPIC_FUTURE_TOKEN", True),  # forward-proofing
38	        ("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", False),  # not a credential
39	        ("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", False),  # subscription auth — keep
40	        ("PATH", False),
41	    ],
42	)
43	def test_is_metered_credential_matches_shape(key, metered):
44	    assert is_metered_anthropic_credential(key) is metered
45	
46	
47	def test_scrubbed_env_strips_metered_keeps_subscription(monkeypatch):
48	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "sk-remove")
49	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_FUTURE_TOKEN", "remove")
50	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:1234")
51	    monkeypatch.setenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "keep")
52	    env = scrubbed_env()
53	    assert "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" not in env
54	    assert "ANTHROPIC_FUTURE_TOKEN" not in env
55	    assert env.get("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL") == "http://localhost:1234"
56	    assert env.get("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN") == "keep"
57	
58	
59	# ---- JSON extraction / balanced-brace walker ----
60

content.text:
Interesting and important: line 38-39 of the test explicitly documents `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` as `False` ("not a credential") and the test at line 50/55 explicitly asserts that `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is KEPT even when set. So this is a deliberate, tested design choice — but a flawed one from a data-sovereignty standpoint.

The key question for severity is whether the threat model is realistic. The attack requires an attacker to control the process environment. If an attacker already controls env vars, this is local-first software on a dedicated box. But the module's own docstring and ADR-0003 elevate "data sovereignty" and "no metered API" to hard invariants the module exists to enforce. The scrubber is explicitly the enforcement mechanism. Let me confirm the CLI honors these vars, which I know from the claude-agent-sdk/Claude Code documentation: `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK`, `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX` are all real, documented Claude Code settings. Let me double-check the SDK passes env to the subprocess.

# find where env from options is applied to subprocess
import claude_agent_sdk._internal.transport.subprocess_cli as sp
src = inspect.getsource(sp)
import re
for i,line in enumerate(src.splitlines()):
    if 'env' in line.lower() and ('options' in line.lower() or 'environ' in line.lower() or 'process' in line.lower() or 'open_process' in line.lower()):
        print(i, line)
" 2>&1 | head -40

content.text:
This is a critical finding within the finding. The SDK at line 430-435 builds `process_env` as `{**inherited_env, ..., **self._options.env, ...}`. This means:

1. `self._options.env` (the `scrubbed_env()` dict) does NOT replace the child env — it is **merged on top of** the full inherited `os.environ`.
2. `scrubbed_env()` returns a dict that **omits** the metered keys (e.g., `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). But because the SDK starts from `inherited_env` (the full unscrubbed `os.environ`) and then overlays `options.env`, an omitted key is NOT removed — the inherited value survives.

Wait — let me verify this carefully. `scrubbed_env()` returns `os.environ` minus metered keys. `inherited_env` = `os.environ` minus `CLAUDECODE`. The merge `{**inherited_env, **options.env}` — for `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`: it's in `inherited_env` (value present) and absent from `options.env`, so the merged result KEEPS the inherited `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. The scrubbing is completely defeated by this SDK version's merge semantics. Let me prove this.

os.environ['CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN']='sub-keep'

# Replicate the SDK merge exactly (subprocess_cli.py L429-435):

process_env = {**inherited_env, 'CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT':'sdk-py', **opt_env, 'CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_VERSION': v}
print('FINAL child env ANTHROPIC_API_KEY =', process_env.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'))
print('FINAL child env ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN =', process_env.get('ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN'))
"

content.text:
This is even worse than the original finding claimed. The original finding said the scrubber strips metered KEY/TOKEN vars but leaves the redirect/provider switches. My PoC proves that against the installed SDK (0.2.102), the scrubber doesn't even strip the metered keys it claims to — because the SDK merges `options.env` ON TOP of the full inherited `os.environ` rather than using it as a replacement. An omitted key falls through to its inherited value.

So both invariants the module exists to enforce are broken against the real installed dependency:
1. The metered-key removal (the module's primary documented purpose) is a no-op — `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` reaches the subprocess.
2. The redirect/provider switches (`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK`, etc.) are forwarded regardless.

The correct fix is a strict allowlist (build a fresh env), which works regardless of merge semantics because allowlisted-only means any non-allowlisted inherited var that the SDK leaves would still be present — wait, no. The allowlist as `options.env` would ALSO be merged on top of inherited env, so non-listed inherited vars STILL leak. The only robust fix is for the caller to either (a) clear the dangerous vars from `os.environ` itself before the call, or (b) explicitly set the dangerous vars to empty/removed in `options.env`. A denylist that sets removed vars is actually needed, OR the engine must mutate its own process environment. This makes the "strict allowlist via options.env" suggestion insufficient given this SDK's merge behavior.

Let me confirm the finding's claim about whether the original assessor noticed the merge — they assumed `options.env` replaces. Regardless, the finding's conclusion (off-box redirect + metered bypass) holds and is actually understated. Let me verify there's no other gating (e.g., the engine clearing os.environ elsewhere).

content.text:
No env clearing anywhere. The seam is the only place handling the subprocess env, and it relies entirely on `scrubbed_env()` passed via `options.env`, which (a) is a denylist not allowlist, and (b) is defeated by the SDK's merge-over-inherited semantics for the very keys it removes.

I have conclusive evidence. The finding is real. The original assessor's two claims (off-box redirect, metered-provider bypass) are confirmed, and I found the impact is actually broader: the scrubber's primary stated purpose (removing the metered `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`) is also defeated by this SDK version.

Severity assessment: This is the security/data-sovereignty enforcement module — its entire reason to exist is these two hard invariants. Both are bypassable. The high-stakes prompt is full analysis content. However, exploitation requires an attacker to already control the process's environment variables on a local-first, single-tenant dedicated box. That's a meaningful precondition that caps this below CRITICAL (no remote/unprivileged trigger). But given this is the dedicated enforcement boundary, it is squarely a HIGH production-readiness defect — the control is non-functional against the installed dependency.

input.reasoning:
CONFIRMED, and the real impact is broader than the original report.

Code facts (src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py): is_metered_anthropic_credential (L113-120) is a name-based DENYLIST that matches only ANTHROPIC_* vars containing 'KEY' or 'TOKEN'. scrubbed_env() (L123-129) returns os.environ minus those keys and nothing else, and is the only env control; it is handed to the subprocess via ClaudeAgentOptions(env=scrubbed_env()) at L336. No code anywhere in src/ clears or mutates os.environ before the call (grep confirmed).

PoC #1 (venv, claude-agent-sdk 0.2.102): scrubbed_env() KEPT every redirect/metered switch — ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, ALL_PROXY, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS — and only DROPPED ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN. These are real, documented Claude Code env settings: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL redirects where the subprocess sends the (full analysis) prompt off-box (ADR-0003 data-sovereignty bypass / exfiltration channel); CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK/USE_VERTEX route through metered AWS Bedrock / GCP Vertex (the 'no metered API' invariant bypass) with the accompanying AWS_*/GOOGLE_* creds forwarded too.

PoC #2 — additional defect I found by reading the SDK source: claude_agent_sdk 0.2.102 subprocess_cli.py (L429-435) builds the child env as process_env = {**inherited_env, ...defaults, **options.env}, i.e. it MERGES options.env ON TOP of the full inherited os.environ rather than using it as the child env. Because scrubbed_env() *omits* the metered keys rather than setting them empty, the omitted key falls through to its inherited value. Replicating the exact merge proved ANTHROPIC_API_KEY='sk-metered-SHOULD-BE-REMOVED' and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN both survive into the final child env even though scrubbed_env() correctly excluded them. So the module's PRIMARY documented purpose — stripping the metered Anthropic key before the subprocess sees it — is itself a no-op against the installed dependency.

Note: tests/test_reasoning.py L38-39 and L50/55 deliberately assert ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is 'not a credential' and is KEPT, so the redirect leak is a documented/intended design choice — but it directly contradicts the module's own docstring and ADR-0003, and the test never exercises the actual SDK merge, so it gives false confidence that the metered key is stripped.

This is the single enforcement boundary for the two hard invariants the module exists to uphold, and both are bypassable. Exploitation requires control of the process environment on a local-first single-tenant box (no remote/unprivileged trigger), which is why this is HIGH rather than CRITICAL.

Fix note: the original 'strict allowlist via options.env' suggestion is INSUFFICIENT given this SDK's merge semantics — any non-listed inherited var still leaks. A correct fix must either (a) os.environ.pop the dangerous vars in the engine process before the call, or (b) explicitly overwrite each dangerous var (including the metered KEY/TOKEN, base-URL, proxy, and provider-switch vars) to a removed/empty sentinel inside options.env, AND route any custom on-box base URL through require_loopback_url (already available in _local.py but unused here).
input.evidence: Read src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py (is_metered_anthropic_credential L113-120, scrubbed_env L123-129, env=scrubbed_env() at L336). Read src/assay_engine/_local.py (require_loopback_url exists but is NOT applied to subprocess env). Read tests/test_reasoning.py L38-56 (explicitly asserts ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL kept — documented design choice). grep over src/ confirmed no os.environ mutation/clearing anywhere. PoC #1 against /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv: scrubbed_env() returned all redirect/metered/proxy/cloud-cred vars KEPT. PoC #2: read claude_agent_sdk 0.2.102 _internal/transport/subprocess_cli.py L429-435 showing process_env = {**inherited_env, ..., **self._options.env, ...} (merge over full os.environ), then replicated the merge to prove ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN survive into the child env despite scrubbed_env() excluding them — the scrubber's primary purpose is defeated by the installed SDK. SDK version confirmed 0.2.102 via pip show.

content.text:
I've completed my independent verification and submitted my structured finding.

Summary: The finding is REAL (HIGH severity). I confirmed via PoC against the venv that `scrubbed_env()` in `/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` (L113-129) is a name-based denylist that forwards `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK/VERTEX`, proxy vars, and cloud creds verbatim to the subprocess — breaking both the data-sovereignty and no-metered-API invariants the module exists to enforce.

Additionally, by reading the installed `claude-agent-sdk` 0.2.102 source (`subprocess_cli.py` L429-435), I found the impact is broader than originally reported: the SDK merges `options.env` on top of the full inherited `os.environ`, so omitting a key (as `scrubbed_env()` does) lets the inherited value fall through. My PoC proved that even `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — the metered key the module's primary purpose is to strip — survives into the child env. The control is effectively non-functional against the installed dependency, which is why the "strict allowlist via options.env" fix alone is insufficient (the engine must pop/overwrite the dangerous vars, not merely omit them).
